clues: Often depicted in Renaissance art with 'da Vinci Sign', possibly as a stylized beauty ideal or reflection of high social status
As early as 1600BC, Chinese physicians were using seaweed and burnt sponge as treatment options, probably effective due to the iodine content
After salt iodization, this condition is frequently only seen in autoimmune conditions now
Nucleosides found in the 1950s in a Caribbean sponge, Cryptotethya crypta ultimately led to the development of this drug and approval in 1981
Because this medication is activated only inside affected cells by thymidine kinase, it was the first drug to destroy viruses without harming host cells
Despite being discovered decades ago, this remains the most widely prescribed antiviral for multiple indications
Initially only diagnosed when severe and life threatening, the advent of new testing has led to most cases being detected in asymptomatic patients now
In advanced cases, benign lytic lesions can form often in the jaws or long bones
In the outpatient setting, this is the most common cause of hypercalcemia
Historically affecting patients in their mid-life in the mid-20th century, this is now primarily considered a disease of the elderly
Mortality rates of this are cited at 50-60% within 2 years of symptom onset but if treated, the 2 year survival rises to 90%
Initially diagnosed by X-ray, this is now frequently found on ultrasound instead

This medication is on the World Anti-Doping Agency's list of banned substances as it can mask the presence of other performance-enhancing drugs although still used extensively in horse racing to treat exercise-induced pulmonary hemorrhage
Available subcutaneously since 2022, this medication is used in about 14% of admitted patients
Acting specifically on the ascending limb of the loop of Henle, this medication replaced less effecive and toxic mercury derived options
